name: Note Lane
description: Note Lane is a timing mechanic where player input, scoring, or simulation aligns with musical or beat-based windows.
design_notes: Note Lane should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For rhythm mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use note lane as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect note lane to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels synchronized with music, timing, and responsive feedback.

parameters.name: perfect_window
parameters.description: Timing tolerance for the best outcome.

parameters.name: miss_penalty
parameters.description: Penalty applied when input falls outside accepted timing.

edge_cases: Note Lane is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Note Lane overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Note Lane runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Note Lane state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Note Lane plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Note Lane produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Note Lane strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Note Lane becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Note Lane with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Note Lane as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Note Lane with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Note Lane as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
